success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success and all expected nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-mode adapter strategy was feasible (import_feasibility 0.72) and allowed MCP plugin startup with healthy service status.
Repository structure was successfully recovered via zip fallback, enabling analysis despite upstream access/method constraints.
Generated MCP scaffold included required runtime files (entrypoint, adapter, service, main, requirements, README), allowing runnable service packaging.
failure_reasons: DeepWiki enrichment failed (external analysis dependency unavailable), reducing documentation/semantic depth of automated analysis.
Original project test status is failed/untested in practice (passed=false with empty test files/details), so core repo correctness was not validated.
Telemetry quality gap: resource/performance metrics were effectively absent (all zeros/empty), limiting confidence in runtime assessment.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing not provided. Zip fallback import indicates resilient but potentially slower retrieval path.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed with medium confidence and useful module/CLI/dependency extraction; DeepWiki sub-step failed, likely increasing reliance on heuristic/static inference.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; 7 MCP output files created. However generated LOC/tool endpoint counters are inconsistent (reported as 0 despite files/endpoints existing), suggesting instrumentation mismatch.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed (stdio, healthy). Original project tests not meaningfully executed; validation depth is shallow.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown due to missing telemetry (reported 0 MB). Cannot claim efficiency.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate expected footprint (small scaffold + existing repo), but exact usage unavailable.
